questions.question: What is the time complexity of a stack data structure?
questions.explanation: A stack follows the Last-In-First-Out (LIFO) principle. The time complexity of stack operations (insertion and deletion at the top) is O(n) in the worst case scenario.

questions.question: Which of the following is a method of sorting using the QuickSort algorithm?
questions.explanation: Partition is a key method used in the QuickSort algorithm to divide the array into smaller sub-arrays.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the 'top' element in a stack data structure?
options: The top element is the first element in the stack.
The top element is the last element in the stack.
The top element is used to keep track of the size of the stack.
The top element is used to keep track of the last operation performed.
questions.explanation: The top element in a stack data structure represents the most recently added element. It is the first element to be popped out of the stack.

questions.question: Which data structure is similar to a set, but allows duplicate elements?
questions.explanation: A set data structure is similar to a stack, but it does not allow duplicate elements. A list data structure, on the other hand, allows duplicate elements.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the 'peek' method in a stack data structure?
options: To add an element to the stack.
To remove an element from the stack.
To get the top element without removing it.
To check if the stack is empty.
questions.explanation: The peek method in a stack data structure allows you to access the top element without removing it. This is useful for getting the top element without affecting the rest of the stack.
